narrative:
Very heavy rainfall has occurred along the Blue Ridge areas of Allegheny County this morning and flooding is likely in areas around Roaring Gap and Sparta. Our station in Roaring Gap has recorded over 6 and 1/2 inches of rain since early this morning and our Sparta station has recorded close to 3 and 1/2 inches.

Slow moving showers and maybe some thunder continue to move slowly this morning, especially across the Blue Ridge. Heavy rain is likely in spots. Western areas see less rain. Rain begins to wind down this afternoon but a threat of showers continues into the evening. Temperatures remain cool most of the day today. Tuesday is much nicer with below average temperatures continuing and only isolated showers, mainly during the morning. Temperatures begin to warm up Wednesday but we remain dry, with only isolated showers or thundershowers on Thursday.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is here and being delivered to our retail outlets. Thanks for a record-breaking pre-publication sale! You can still avoid shipping costs with our pickup events beginning Thursday, July 2, in Boone. We'll take that to Banner Elk, West Jefferson, Spruce Pine, and Black Mountain next week; click here for details. You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page . Check out volume discounts on the calendar and the "Mix and Match" sale on Drinkware.

Monday Soaker

Forecast Discussion

A stalled front to the south and area of low pressure in eastern TN are aiding the development of rain and some thunder across the High Country this morning. Very heavy rainfall has occurred around Roaring Gap and Sparta since early this morning resulting in flooding in many places. Rainfall rates over 2 inches per hour are occurring in the areas of heaviest rain. Our station in Roaring Gap has recorded over 6.5 inches of rain since around 3 am this morning. Our Sparta station has recorded over 3.5 inches.

Slow moving showers and maybe some thunder continue this morning into the early afternoon, especially along the Blue Ridge. Heavy rain is likely in spots. Western areas see less rain. Shower coverage and intensity begin to decrease during the afternoon.

Temperatures are kept well below average by the clouds and rain. Even tonight is a bit cool as cooler air works in.

Cooler and drier air move in tomorrow as high pressure builds in from the north. There is a risk of isolated showers in the morning but much of the day is dry with slightly below average temperatures.

Temperatures warm back up Wednesday and Thursday as the flow turns southwest. Wednesday should be dry but isolated showers and thundershowers are possible Thursday.

A cold front moves our way from the north late in the week but fades as it reaches us. It will increase the coverage of afternoon and evening showers and thunderstorms. Another cold front approaches from the north late in the weekend increasing the coverage of afternoon/evening showers and thunderstorms. Temperatures are close to average.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Jul 08, 2026
Monday Message for July 6, 2026

It's the first Monday Message of July! Mercifully, temperatures are cooler this week, although rainfall continues to be scattered and somewhat unreliable. We also take a look back at June rainfall and temperatures.

Jul 06, 2026
Monday Message for June 29, 2026

It's the Independence Day / America 250 edition of the Monday Message, available to subscribers. This week, it's all about heat, humidity and isolated afternoon thunderstorms.
